Once Upon a Time…in Hollywood (2019)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $142,502,728Details
International Box Office $234,924,175
Worldwide Box Office $377,426,903
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $10,062,206
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $20,665,216
Total $30,727,422
Further financial details...

Synopsis

1969 Los Angeles, where everything is changing, as TV star Rick Dalton and his longtime stunt double Cliff Booth make their way around an industry they hardly recognize anymore.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$41,082,018 (28.8% of total gross)
Legs:3.47 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:37.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$90,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,659 opening theaters/3,659 max. theaters, 7.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $186,058,467

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 26th, 2019 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: July 24th, 2019 (Wide) (Taiwan)
July 25th, 2019 (Wide) (Hong Kong)
August 8th, 2019 (Wide), released as Однажды в Голливуде (Russia (CIS))
August 14th, 2019 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
August 14th, 2019 (Wide) (Iceland)
Video Release: November 26th, 2019 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
December 9th, 2019 by Sony Pictures, released as Once Upon a Time in… Hollywood
MPA Rating: R for language throughout, some strong graphic violence, drug use, and sexual references.
(Rating bulletin 2587 (Cert #52050), 7/17/2019)
Running Time: 159 minutes
Award nomination: 2020 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Critics’ Choice Winner Best Picture, Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Musical or Comedy, Golden Globe Winner Best Screenplay—Motion Picture
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Hollywood, Los Angeles
Plot point: Addiction, Aspiring Actress / Actor, Stuntman, TV Actor
Social setting: TV Industry
Source material: Inspired by a True Story
Subgenre: Crime Thriller
Time period setting: 1960s
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, Heyday Films, Bona Film Group
Production Countries: United States, United Kingdom
Languages: English

  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

1969 Los Angeles, where everything is changing, as TV star Rick Dalton and his longtime stunt double Cliff Booth make their way around an industry they hardly recognize anymore.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$41,082,018 (28.8% of total gross)
Legs:3.47 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:37.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)

Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $186,058,467
